name: Two Pointers
description: Two indices traversing a sequence. Use for sorted-array problems, palindrome checks, and pair-sum variants.

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintain a window of elements over a sequence. Use for subarray/substring problems with a size or constraint.

name: Binary Search
description: Halve the search space each step. Works on sorted arrays and on answer spaces (binary search the answer).

name: DFS
description: Depth-first traversal via recursion or an explicit stack. Use for tree/graph exploration and path-building.

name: BFS
description: Breadth-first traversal via a queue. Use for shortest path on unweighted graphs and level-order tree traversal.

name: Backtracking
description: Recursive search with state restoration on each return. Use for permutations, combinations, and N-queens-style problems.

name: Dynamic Programming
description: Cache subproblem results. Use for optimal substructure: knapsack, LIS, edit distance, grid paths.

name: Hash Map
description: O(1) lookups for membership and counting. Use for two-sum variants, anagrams, and frequency tracking.
